skill.name: internationalization-helper
skill.description: Helps extract hardcoded user-facing strings, organize translation keys, and check locale coverage for multi-language applications.
skill.summary: Find, organize, and validate translation work for i18n projects.
skill.author: CrazyDubya

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and blocker patterns, but review shows most are markdown code fences, translation examples, or security-related words in sample locale text. One fixed local copy command appears in documentation as a template creation example; it is low risk and has no dynamic input, network access, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Fixed Local Copy Command in Documentation
low_findings.description: The bash example copies a base locale file to a new locale file. This is an external command pattern, but it uses fixed local paths and has no user-controlled shell input, network access, credential access, or hidden execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is present, but it is a static documentation example for local file copying. The low score reflects limited security impact and no injection vector.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution findings are markdown fences and inline documentation examples. They describe JavaScript, JSON, PO files, output examples, and CSS guidance rather than executable backtick calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static markdown examples, not code executed by the skill. No command substitution or runtime shell path is present in those locations.

low_findings.title: Security Keywords Misread as Crypto or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match i18n metadata, translation key examples, and password-related UI text. No cryptographic algorithm, system probing logic, or host reconnaissance behavior appears in the cited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is human-readable localization guidance and sample authentication copy. There is no evidence of hashing, encryption, host enumeration, or system information collection.

content.value_statement: Multi-language apps often ship with missing translations and inconsistent locale files. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find strings, organize keys, and review coverage.
actual_capabilities: Identifies common i18n frameworks for JavaScript, Vue, Python, and Ruby projects.
Finds user-facing hardcoded strings in templates, forms, errors, and buttons.
Suggests structured translation keys grouped by feature or common UI area.
Compares locale files and reports missing translation keys.
Guides pluralization, date formatting, number formatting, and RTL support.
Provides a sample locale file structure for common app text.
limitations: It does not translate content automatically or verify translation quality.
It does not include runnable extraction scripts or framework-specific plugins.
It needs project context to know the active i18n library and file layout.
It may require human review for tone, legal text, and culturally sensitive copy.

use_cases.title: Prepare a React App for Localization
use_cases.description: Find hardcoded JSX text, propose translation keys, and move strings into locale files.

use_cases.title: Audit Translation Coverage Before Release
use_cases.description: Compare locale files and list missing keys before a multi-language launch.

use_cases.title: Standardize Translation Key Naming
use_cases.description: Review existing keys and group them by feature, shared UI, and error context.

prompt_templates.title: Find Hardcoded Strings
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one screen or component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component for user-facing hardcoded strings. List each string and suggest clear i18n keys.

prompt_templates.title: Create Locale Entries
prompt_templates.scenario: Move approved strings into translation files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract these UI strings into the existing locale structure. Keep keys grouped by feature and preserve variables.

prompt_templates.title: Check Locale Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Release readiness check for several languages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the base locale with the other locale files. Report missing keys, extra keys, and placeholder mismatches.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an i18n Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this project layout and propose an i18n migration plan. Include framework choice, file structure, extraction order, and review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: A checkout page contains hardcoded labels, validation messages, and button text.
output: A grouped list of strings that need translation.
Suggested keys for checkout actions, form labels, and validation errors.
Notes about variables that translators must preserve.

output_examples.input: The English locale has more keys than the Spanish and French locale files.
output: A list of missing keys for each language.
Warnings for placeholder mismatches and unused keys.

output_examples.input: A product page needs plural counts, currency, dates, and RTL support.
output: Guidance for plural forms and count placeholders.
Recommendations for locale-aware number and date formatting.

best_practices: Use stable keys that describe purpose, not the exact source sentence.
Keep placeholders consistent across every locale file.
Review translations in the real interface with long text and RTL languages.
anti_patterns: Do not concatenate translated sentence fragments in application code.
Do not use source text as the only translation key for complex apps.
Do not ship new features before checking locale coverage and placeholders.

faq.question: Does this skill translate text for me?
faq.answer: No. It organizes translation work and highlights coverage gaps. Human or professional translation is still needed.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it recognize?
faq.answer: It covers common JavaScript, Vue, Python, and Ruby i18n patterns, including i18next, FormatJS, gettext, Django, and Rails.

faq.question: Can it check missing locale keys?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare a base locale with other locale files and report missing or extra keys.

faq.question: Does it support pluralization?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives guidance for plural forms and count placeholders, but framework-specific rules still need validation.

faq.question: Can it help with right-to-left languages?
faq.answer: Yes. It reminds teams to check direction settings, logical CSS properties, and layout behavior for RTL languages.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found only low-risk documentation patterns. It does not contain network calls, secret access, or malicious instructions.
